face_visibility.notes: Face is visible enough for review but remains low-poly and partially affected by cue overlays.

mouth_movement_readability.notes: Mouth cue is visible during extended dialogue playback, but it remains a procedural cue rather than mesh or morph-target lip-sync.

gaze_target_alignment.notes: Eye-focus cues are less intrusive and support target review, but gaze remains cue-based rather than skeletal/ocular animation.

pose_naturalness.notes: Standing pose remains mannequin-like with visible stiffness in shoulders and torso.

hand_arm_naturalness.notes: Arms and hands remain visibly rigid and partly over-rotated; next slice reduces arm abduction and hand offset.

recommendation: Keep runtime gate blocked for learner-use until authored/retargeted upper-limb idle animation or better bone posture evidence improves hand and arm naturalness.
